title: review staleness reconciliation before merge
purpose: G4.4 defines review staleness reconciliation before merge for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake.
scope: Owns `G4.4` only; siblings under `G4` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §9 — Branch G — Verification & quality plane (anti-mistake)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#9-branch-g-verification-quality-plane-anti-mistake).
behavior_steps: On each pursuit wake, `route-tier.py` reads `capability_class` (S0–S4) from `state.json` and assigns genius conductor versus economy worker roles per B1 before any implement turn runs.
The genius-tier parent orchestrates thin turns only—spawning economy workers for bounded task cards per B2.1 and B3.1 when `spawn_workers` is true.
B1.1 runs mandatory `scripts/` output before improvisation whenever file-derived behavior exists; Plane B enforces this ahead of LLM routing on every wake.

Model tier selection from `docs/operator/model-policy.json` maps conductor and worker models to pursuit cost and risk class per B3, while B4 interleaves product `next_action` steps with platform promotion queue peeks.
If routing would assign S4 governance work to economy workers or skip S0 when a script exists, pursuit stops at H2 with a capability-class violation until the conductor realigns with model policy.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Flaky test â€” escalation S4 once, then H2 with evidence log; no silent retry loop.
Edge case `G4.4` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `G4.4`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Extend verify-router for goal-level suite invocation.
Wire CI: validate-workflow checks goal block when pursuit.mode=goal_autopilot.
Validate `G4.4` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `G4.4` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `G4.4`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: G4.4 defines review staleness reconciliation before merge for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake. In practice, the system on each pursuit wake, `route-tier.py` reads `capability_class` (s0–s4) from `state.json` and assigns genius conductor versus economy worker roles per b1 before any implement turn runs, then the genius-tier parent orchestrates thin turns only—spawning economy workers for bounded task cards per b2.1 and b3.1 when `spawn_workers` is true.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-g 2026-06-28 -->

Before merge or program integration, staleness reconciliation must show design artifacts current: `docs/manifest/staleness.json` and reconcile-stale/reconcile-artifact-graph outputs gate git-workflow when upstream HLD, DD, or manifest nodes changed.

Review at merge time catches implement against stale specs—a mistake class mapped in [G5.5](G5.5-mistake-stale-design-staleness-reconcile.md). Conductor runs S0 staleness scripts; workers do not waive merge on stale graph nodes without operator approval.
